---
title: "PMHD Ooko Draft"
author: "Gruop 16"
date: "2026-04-14"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```



```{r, include=FALSE}

# -----------------------------
# 1. Load packages
# -----------------------------
packages <- c(
  "tidyverse",
  "lme4",
  "lmerTest",
  "emmeans",
  "performance",
  "DHARMa",
  "ggplot2",
   "rsample",
   "yardstick",
   "purrr"
)

installed <- rownames(installed.packages())
for (p in packages) {
  if (!p %in% installed) install.packages(p)
}
lapply(packages, library, character.only = TRUE)
```

```{r}

# -----------------------------
# 2. Read the data
# -----------------------------
outcome <- read.csv("C:/Users/guest441/Desktop/Hasselt/PMHD/G16.outcome.data.csv", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
size <- read.csv("C:/Users/guest441/Desktop/Hasselt/PMHD/G16.size.data.csv", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
soil <- read.csv("C:/Users/guest441/Desktop/Hasselt/PMHD/G16.soil.data.csv", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
```



```{r}
# -----------------------------
# 3. Clean and prepare variables
# -----------------------------
outcome <- outcome %>%
  mutate(
    climate  = factor(climate),
    species  = factor(species),
    location = factor(location),
    ecotr_id = factor(ecotr_id),
    tree_id  = factor(tree_id),
    pear_id  = factor(pear_id)
  )

# Set reference levels
# Climate reference = Scenario 1 (control/reference condition)
# Species reference = Conference (change if you prefer the other one)


outcome$climate <- relevel(outcome$climate, ref = "Scenario 1")
outcome$species <- relevel(outcome$species, ref = "Conference")

unique(outcome$climate)
unique(outcome$species)
```

```{r}

# -----------------------------
# 4. Basic exploratory checks
# -----------------------------
options(na.print = "NA")

# Sample sizes by climate and species
sample_table <- table(outcome$climate, outcome$species)
sample_table

# Number of pears per tree
pear_counts <- outcome %>%
  count(ecotr_id, tree_id, climate, species, name = "n_pears") %>%
  arrange(desc(n_pears))

head(pear_counts, 20)

# Missing values
missing_summary <- colSums(is.na(outcome))
missing_summary

# Distribution of quality score
ggplot(outcome, aes(x = quality_idx)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 30) +
  labs(
    title = "Distribution of Quality Score",
    x = "Quality Index",
    y = "Count"
  )

# Boxplot by climate
ggplot(outcome, aes(x = climate, y = quality_idx)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(
    title = "Quality Score by Climate",
    x = "Climate Scenario",
    y = "Quality Index"
  )

# Boxplot by climate and species
ggplot(outcome, aes(x = climate, y = quality_idx, fill = species)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(
    title = "Quality Score by Climate and Species",
    x = "Climate Scenario",
    y = "Quality Index"
  )

```

# Inferential model (Should be the primary modedl)

```{r}
# Set reference categories
outcome$climate <- relevel(outcome$climate, ref = "Scenario 1")
outcome$species <- relevel(outcome$species, ref = "Conference")

# Fit model
m_primary <- lmer(
  quality_idx ~ climate * species + (1 | tree_id),
  data = outcome,
  REML = TRUE
)

# Output
summary(m_primary)
anova(m_primary, type = 3)
```

# Predictive model (secondary)
# 5-Fold CV models
```{r}
# Set references
outcome$climate <- relevel(outcome$climate, ref = "Scenario 1")
outcome$species <- relevel(outcome$species, ref = "Conference")

# Check
table(outcome$climate, outcome$species)
```

```{r}
m_base <- lmer(
  quality_idx ~ climate * species + (1 | tree_id),
  data = outcome,
  REML = TRUE
)

summary(m_base)
anova(m_base, type = 3)
```

```{r}
set.seed(123)

folds <- group_vfold_cv(
  outcome,
  group = tree_id,
  v = 5
)
```


```{r}
candidate_models <- list(
  m1 = quality_idx ~ climate + species + (1 | tree_id),
  m2 = quality_idx ~ climate * species + (1 | tree_id)
)
```

```{r}
fit_and_evaluate <- function(split, formula) {
  
  train <- analysis(split)
  test  <- assessment(split)
  
  model <- lmer(
    formula,
    data = train,
    REML = FALSE
  )
  
  preds <- predict(
    model,
    newdata = test,
    allow.new.levels = TRUE
  )
  
  tibble(
    rmse = rmse_vec(test$quality_idx, preds),
    mae  = mae_vec(test$quality_idx, preds),
    rsq  = rsq_vec(test$quality_idx, preds)
  )
}
```

```{r}
cv_results <- map_dfr(names(candidate_models), function(name) {
  
  formula <- candidate_models[[name]]
  
  results <- map_dfr(folds$splits, fit_and_evaluate, formula = formula)
  
  results %>%
    mutate(model = name, .before = 1)
})

cv_results
```

```{r}
cv_summary <- cv_results %>%
  group_by(model) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_rmse = mean(rmse),
    sd_rmse   = sd(rmse),
    mean_mae  = mean(mae),
    mean_rsq  = mean(rsq),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(mean_rmse)

cv_summary
```

```{r}
best_model_name <- cv_summary %>%
  slice(1) %>%
  pull(model)

best_model_name
```

```{r}
final_formula <- candidate_models[[best_model_name]]

m_final <- lmer(
  final_formula,
  data = outcome,
  REML = TRUE
)

summary(m_final)
```
